notes.note: The petitio consulatus was the formal candidacy and electioneering for the consulship; Paullus has now passed from candidate to consul-designate to consul of 50 BC.

notes.anchor: the senate's decree on my achievements be made in the most honorific terms
notes.note: A senatus consultum praising a commander's res gestae was the formal prelude to a public thanksgiving and ultimately a triumph; Cicero is lobbying the consul to steer the decree on his Cilician campaign through the Senate in flattering terms.

notes.anchor: worthy of honour and public thanksgiving
notes.note: The supplicatio was a formal period of public thanksgiving to the gods voted by the Senate to honour a general's victory; it counted as official recognition and was a normal precondition for a later triumph. Cicero secured one for his Amanus campaign.

notes.anchor: the official dispatches I have sent to you, to your colleague, and to the Senate
notes.note: Paullus's consular colleague for 50 BC was C. Claudius Marcellus; commanders reported their victories by official letter (litterae publicae) to both consuls and to the Senate to win recognition.
